title: ED diarrhea documentation
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

presenting_history.text: Defines acute diarrheal illness and records stool frequency, duration, nausea, vomiting, fever, abdominal pain, exposure pattern, and dehydration features.
presenting_history.rationale: The exact DHA adult gastroenteritis history section supports onset, duration, severity, associated symptoms, relevant negatives and risk context for adult diarrhea without diagnosing gastroenteritis.
presenting_history.evidence_extract: Defines acute diarrheal illness and records stool frequency, duration, nausea, vomiting, fever, abdominal pain, exposure pattern, and dehydration features.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Gastroenteritis in Adults – 41

red_flags.text: Lists shock, severe dehydration, intractable vomiting, severe abdominal pain, prolonged symptoms, older age, comorbidity, pregnancy, antibiotic exposure, nocturnal diarrhea, and bloody or black stool.
red_flags.rationale: The exact red-flag section supports measured vital data, general appearance and time-critical symptoms only when assessed.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Lists shock, severe dehydration, intractable vomiting, severe abdominal pain, prolonged symptoms, older age, comorbidity, pregnancy, antibiotic exposure, nocturnal diarrhea, and bloody or black stool.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Gastroenteritis in Adults – 41

red_flags.text: Defines emergency and face-to-face referral features for adult gastroenteritis.
red_flags.rationale: The exact referral section supports recording escalation, specialist discussion or handover only when already decided or arranged.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Defines emergency and face-to-face referral features for adult gastroenteritis.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Gastroenteritis in Adults – 41

red_flags.text: Defines adult gastroenteritis use in DHA-licensed telehealth and excludes emergency and controlled-medication scenarios.
red_flags.rationale: The adult virtual-care scope was reviewed; pediatric, chronic, inflammatory, surgical and immunocompromised presentations remain outside direct scope.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Defines adult gastroenteritis use in DHA-licensed telehealth and excludes emergency and controlled-medication scenarios.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Gastroenteritis in Adults – 41

investigations.text: Describes history-based assessment and investigation for suspected nonviral or complicated gastroenteritis.
investigations.rationale: The exact investigation section supports recording existing test results reviewed by the clinician without recommending testing.
investigations.evidence_extract: Describes history-based assessment and investigation for suspected nonviral or complicated gastroenteritis.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Gastroenteritis in Adults – 41

source_limitations: Frequency, volume, blood or mucus, vomiting, hydration, travel, exposure, outbreak, medicine and antibiotic history, abdominal examination, diagnosis, stool testing, treatment and disposition remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
